Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Copyright Act (diagnosis, maintenance or repair)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Copyright Act (diagnosis, maintenance or repair)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Copyright Act in order to allow the circumvention of a technological protection measure in a computer program if the circumvention is solely for the purpose of diagnosis, maintenance or repair of a product in which the program is embedded. It also allows the manufacture, importation, distribution, sale, renting and provision of technologies, devices or components used for diagnosis, maintenance or repair of such products.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition circumvent in section 41 of the Copyright Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a technological protection measure within the meaning of paragraph (a) of the definition technological protection measure, to descramble a scrambled work or computer program, or decrypt an encrypted work or computer program or to otherwise avoid, bypass, remove, deactivate or impair the technological protection measure, unless it is done with the authority of the copyright owner; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition technological protection measure in section 41 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: controls access to a work, including a computer program, to a performerâs performance fixed in a sound recording or to a sound recording and whose use is authorized by the copyright owner;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41.12:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 41.1(1)(a) does not apply to a person who circumvents a technological protection measure that controls access to a computer program if the person does so for the sole purpose of diagnosing, maintaining or repairing a product in which the computer program is embedded.

Paragraph 41.1(1)(c) does not apply to a person who manufactures, imports or provides a technology, device or component for the purposes of circumventing a technological protection measure that controls access to a computer program if the person does so for the purpose of diagnosing, maintaining or repairing a product in which the program is embedded and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: uses that technology, device or component only for that purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provides that technology, device or component to another person solely for that purpose.
